# Planting Schedule — 2026 Season

## Indoor Seed Starting

- **February 15**: Start all tomato seeds indoors under grow lights
  - San Marzano: 12 seeds (need 8 plants, extras for insurance)
  - Cherokee Purple: 9 seeds (need 6 plants)
  - Sungold: 6 seeds (need 4 plants — first succession)
  - Early Girl: 9 seeds (need 6 plants)
- **Seed starting mix**: Espoma Organic Seed Starter, moistened before filling 72-cell trays
- **Temperature**: Heat mat set to 80F for germination (expect 5-7 days), reduce to 70F after emergence
- **Lighting**: 16 hours under T5 fluorescent grow lights (Hydrofarm Jump Start), positioned 2 inches above seedlings, raised as plants grow

## Seedling Care (February 22 — March 31)

- **Watering**: Bottom-water every 2-3 days to encourage deep root growth
- **Fertilizing**: Begin at first true leaf stage with fish emulsion (Neptune's Harvest 2-3-1) diluted to half strength, weekly
- **Potting up**: Transplant from 72-cell trays to 4-inch pots when seedlings have 2 sets of true leaves (approximately March 8-12). Bury stem up to first leaves to encourage adventitious root growth.
- **March 15**: Start second succession of Sungold seeds (2 additional plants for May 1 transplant)

## Hardening Off (April 1 — April 14)

Two-week gradual acclimation to outdoor conditions:

- **Days 1-3**: 2 hours outdoors in filtered shade, bring inside at night
- **Days 4-6**: 4 hours outdoors in partial sun
- **Days 7-10**: 6 hours in full sun, leave out overnight if temps stay above 50F
- **Days 11-14**: Full day outdoors, overnight stays (cover with row cover if frost threatens)

## Transplanting

- **April 15**: Transplant all main-season plants to raised beds (coincides with average last frost date for Portland)
  - Soil temperature must be at least 60F at 4-inch depth (check with soil thermometer)
  - Transplant in late afternoon to reduce transplant shock
  - Apply mycorrhizal inoculant to each planting hole
  - Water in with diluted seaweed extract (Maxicrop)
  - Install tomato cages immediately
- **May 1**: Transplant second succession Sungold plants

## Succession Planting

- **May 15**: Direct sow a third batch of Sungold seeds in Bed 3 (2 plants) for late-season harvest extending into October
- **Rationale**: Staggering Sungold plantings by 2 weeks provides continuous cherry tomato harvest from late June through first frost
